title: To the moon you should fly myself only
display_title: To the moon you should fly myself only
wikitext:
[]: To the moon you should fly myself only.             Quote Post by robersora » Mon Aug 13, 2012 5:00 am Hey, I couldn't find anything with a quick search over the web or evageeks, respectively. But if there is such a thread already, have my humblest apologies and close this one immediately. I have come to ask myself, WHY fly me to the moon. Has there ever been made a statement about why they chose this song? I mean, they could have gone with What's up by the 4 non Blonds, but no, they chose Fly me to the Moon... So, if anybody knows, I would appreciate an answer :)

[]: Straight to the moon, I say! BD             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Aug 13, 2012 5:55 am ^There's always the Rei = the Moon symbolism, and the English = Cool factor, which was probably even stronger in the-mid 90's. If the creators we're really on their game with this, it may have even be for the "translation" angle, the narrator says one thing, but really means another. Sure is plenty of that going on within the series.

[]: Re: To the moon you should fly myself only.             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Aug 13, 2012 7:43 am robersora wrote: I mean, they could have gone with What's up by the 4 non Blonds,

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Aug 13, 2012 10:29 am What I would like to know is why there are so many versions of the song. Did it really become that popular?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Aug 13, 2012 1:29 pm The moon was originally planned to play a bigger role in the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Aug 13, 2012 1:46 pm There are some thoughts on ye olde commentary site: http://evacommentary.org/ed/ed.html (Scroll down a little.)

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Aug 13, 2012 5:46 pm AR-99 wrote: . Did it really become that popular?

[]:             Quote Post by evafan76 » Tue Aug 14, 2012 8:58 am Joseph the PRPD wrote: I think Frank Sinatra's cover helped.

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Tue Aug 14, 2012 11:41 am evafan76 wrote: One thing that kinda dissapointed me in the series is Blue Eyes's version wasn't ever used. It would be kinda cool if they found a way to incorporate somehow in Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Aug 14, 2012 3:51 pm It could have been played when Gendo and Fuyutsuki go to Tabgha Base.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Aug 14, 2012 8:01 pm evafan76 wrote: One thing that kinda dissapointed me in the series is Blue Eyes's version wasn't ever used. It would be kinda cool if they found a way to incorporate somehow in Rebuild.

[]: Re: To the moon you should fly myself only.             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Tue Aug 14, 2012 11:21 pm robersora wrote: I have come to ask myself, WHY fly me to the moon. Has there ever been made a statement about why they chose this song? I mean, they could have gone with What's up by the 4 non Blonds, but no, they chose Fly me to the Moon... So, if anybody knows, I would appreciate an answer :)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Aug 15, 2012 10:41 pm NAveryW wrote: The moon was originally planned to play a bigger role in the show.

[]:             Quote Post by juaxoo » Wed Aug 29, 2012 6:10 pm i think anno and other people just liked the song, it does sound good and it suits evangelion as an ending song. the song's meaning could also have something to do with it being used and covered by the voice actors on different occasions.

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Wed Aug 29, 2012 8:14 pm As a few people mentioned Rei seems to always be around the moon so that may be a theory. also they could have found it rather nice and decided to put it in there

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Aug 29, 2012 8:31 pm Reichu wrote: Not so much "planned" as "tentatively proposed". :p
